title: Four Cubby Hutch for the Modular Playroom Storage Wall Collection
summary: A small open-front hutch with a center divider and two shelves, sized to combine with matching pieces into a modular playroom storage wall.
description:
This is a companion piece meant to sit alongside matching modules from the same collection — build it on its own as a stand-alone cubby hutch, or line up several against a playroom wall for a full storage system. The inner pieces (shelves and divider) aren't structural, so a mix of hidden pocket holes on the undersides plus glued brad nails holds everything together without visible fasteners on the front-facing edges.

Squaring the case matters more here than the joinery method: cut the 1/4" plywood back first and use it to true up the whole carcass before nailing it on, rather than trusting the case to already be square. Any piece taller than about waist height should be anchored to a wall stud — this is an open-front cubby with real weight up top, and it can tip forward if a kid climbs it.

materials.name: Pine, 1x12, 8 ft
materials.note: Or a half sheet of 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, ripped into 11-1/4" strips (2 strips needed).

materials.name: Edge banding in matching wood veneer

steps.title: Build the basic outline
steps.body: Build the outline of the hutch using a combination of pocket holes (hidden on undersides) and 1-1/4" brad nails with wood glue.

steps.title: Add the inner shelves and divider
steps.body: The shelves and center divider aren't structural but still need to be tacked in place with brad nails and glue. Where you can't nail from the outside, angle-nail from the center instead.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Cut the 1/4" plywood back and attach it to all the 1x12 pieces with 3/4" brad nails, using the plywood to square up the whole case so its edges match the square-cut back.

steps.title: Add the top trim
steps.body: Add the 1x2 top trim to the insides with wood glue and 1-1/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Add nosing trim and finish
steps.body: Add the 3/4" nosing trim to the top front edge. If installing, tall pieces need to be anchored to wall studs to prevent tipping forward, and multiple modules should be attached together. Sand and finish.

images.alt: A small open-front cubby hutch with a center divider for a modular playroom storage wall
